papers.title: Nutritional specialization and social evolution in woodroaches and termites
papers.abstract: Woodroach biparental-care and termite sibling-altruism evolved from solitary cockroach ancestors following nutritional specialization on nutrient-deficient dead-wood, but the accompanying genomic changes remained unclear. We sequenced eight new Blattodea species showing stepwise contracted genomes. Woodroach brood-rearing remained constrained by deactivated oxidative phosphorylation and peroxisome genes, consistent with slow immature growth. Termites lost key genes mediating sperm motility, corroborating that reproductive division of labor required monogamous colony-founding. They also co-opted many genes from fundamental nutrition-sensitive juvenile hormone, insulin, EGFR and Dpp signaling pathways. Thus, most larvae develop as workers via high energy metabolism early on, while reproductive nymphs highly express energy metabolism genes late in development. These pathways are consistent with obligate dependence on provisioning by specialized workers and feedback loops allowing large homeostatic colonies to evolve.

papers.llm_title_zh: 木蠊与白蚁中的营养特化与社会演化
papers.llm_title_bilingual: 木蠊与白蚁中的营养特化与社会演化 / Nutritional specialization and social evolution in woodroaches and termites
papers.llm_research_question_zh: 营养贫乏枯木上的营养特化如何推动拟白蚁和白蚁社会性起源，并伴随哪些关键基因组与发育调控通路变化？
llm_highlights_zh: 枯木营养特化被认为推动了拟白蚁与白蚁由孤栖蜚蠊祖先向社会性演化。
分步收缩的基因组变化伴随能量代谢、精子运动及发育信号通路的重塑。
结果支持工蚁供养依赖与稳定反馈回路共同促进大型稳态群体形成。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 本研究基于8个新测序的蜚蠊目物种，探讨枯木营养特化背景下拟白蚁与白蚁社会性演化的基因组基础。结果表明，基因组分步收缩以及能量代谢、精子运动和营养敏感性发育信号通路的重塑，可能共同支撑了育幼行为、生殖分工和大型稳态群体的形成。
papers.llm_pubmed_abstract_zh: 拟白蚁的双亲育幼和白蚁的同胞利他行为，起源于孤栖蜚蠊祖先对营养贫乏枯木资源的营养特化，但其伴随的基因组改变此前尚不清楚。作者测序了8个新的蜚蠊目物种，显示出分阶段的基因组收缩。拟白蚁的育幼仍受氧化磷酸化和过氧化物酶体相关基因失活的限制，这与未成熟个体生长缓慢一致。白蚁则丢失了介导精子运动的关键基因，支持生殖分工的形成需要单配偶建群。与此同时，白蚁还重塑并利用了多种来自基础营养敏感性通路的基因，包括幼虫激素、胰岛素、EGFR和Dpp信号通路。因此，多数幼体在发育早期通过较高能量代谢发育为工蚁，而生殖型若虫则在发育后期高表达能量代谢相关基因。这些通路变化与对白蚁专职工级供养的专性依赖以及促进大型稳态群体演化的反馈回路相一致。

all_papers.title: Nutritional specialization and social evolution in woodroaches and termites
all_papers.abstract: Woodroach biparental-care and termite sibling-altruism evolved from solitary cockroach ancestors following nutritional specialization on nutrient-deficient dead-wood, but the accompanying genomic changes remained unclear. We sequenced eight new Blattodea species showing stepwise contracted genomes. Woodroach brood-rearing remained constrained by deactivated oxidative phosphorylation and peroxisome genes, consistent with slow immature growth. Termites lost key genes mediating sperm motility, corroborating that reproductive division of labor required monogamous colony-founding. They also co-opted many genes from fundamental nutrition-sensitive juvenile hormone, insulin, EGFR and Dpp signaling pathways. Thus, most larvae develop as workers via high energy metabolism early on, while reproductive nymphs highly express energy metabolism genes late in development. These pathways are consistent with obligate dependence on provisioning by specialized workers and feedback loops allowing large homeostatic colonies to evolve.

all_papers.llm_title_zh: 木蠊与白蚁中的营养特化与社会演化
all_papers.llm_title_bilingual: 木蠊与白蚁中的营养特化与社会演化 / Nutritional specialization and social evolution in woodroaches and termites
all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: 营养贫乏枯木上的营养特化如何推动拟白蚁和白蚁社会性起源，并伴随哪些关键基因组与发育调控通路变化？
llm_highlights_zh: 枯木营养特化被认为推动了拟白蚁与白蚁由孤栖蜚蠊祖先向社会性演化。
分步收缩的基因组变化伴随能量代谢、精子运动及发育信号通路的重塑。
结果支持工蚁供养依赖与稳定反馈回路共同促进大型稳态群体形成。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 本研究基于8个新测序的蜚蠊目物种，探讨枯木营养特化背景下拟白蚁与白蚁社会性演化的基因组基础。结果表明，基因组分步收缩以及能量代谢、精子运动和营养敏感性发育信号通路的重塑，可能共同支撑了育幼行为、生殖分工和大型稳态群体的形成。
all_papers.llm_pubmed_abstract_zh: 拟白蚁的双亲育幼和白蚁的同胞利他行为，起源于孤栖蜚蠊祖先对营养贫乏枯木资源的营养特化，但其伴随的基因组改变此前尚不清楚。作者测序了8个新的蜚蠊目物种，显示出分阶段的基因组收缩。拟白蚁的育幼仍受氧化磷酸化和过氧化物酶体相关基因失活的限制，这与未成熟个体生长缓慢一致。白蚁则丢失了介导精子运动的关键基因，支持生殖分工的形成需要单配偶建群。与此同时，白蚁还重塑并利用了多种来自基础营养敏感性通路的基因，包括幼虫激素、胰岛素、EGFR和Dpp信号通路。因此，多数幼体在发育早期通过较高能量代谢发育为工蚁，而生殖型若虫则在发育后期高表达能量代谢相关基因。这些通路变化与对白蚁专职工级供养的专性依赖以及促进大型稳态群体演化的反馈回路相一致。

all_papers.primary_summary_raw: Woodroach biparental-care and termite sibling-altruism evolved from solitary cockroach ancestors following nutritional specialization on nutrient-deficient dead-wood, but the accompanying genomic changes remained unclear. We sequenced eight new Blattodea species showing stepwise contracted genomes. Woodroach brood-rearing remained constrained by deactivated oxidative phosphorylation and peroxisome genes, consistent with slow immature growth. Termites lost key genes mediating sperm motility, corroborating that reproductive division of labor required monogamous colony-founding. They also co-opted many genes from fundamental nutrition-sensitive juvenile hormone, insulin, EGFR and Dpp signaling pathways. Thus, most larvae develop as workers via high energy metabolism early on, while reproductive nymphs highly express energy metabolism genes late in development. These pathways are consistent with obligate dependence on provisioning by specialized workers and feedback loops allowing large homeostatic colonies to evolve.
